channel.title: Math on Nate Glenn's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Math on Nate Glenn's Blog

item.title: The Extended Euclidian Algorithm in Perl
item.author: garfieldnate <at> gmail <dot> com (Nathan Glenn)
item.description: This week I learned about the extended Euclidian algorithm for finding a linear combination of two numbers that yields their GCD. For example, the GCD of 213 and 171 is 3, and -4*213 + 5*171 = 3. This algorithm is important in the RSA encryption scheme. I had quite a difficult time getting myself to fully understand how it works. I jumped between Wikipedia, my data structures textbook (don’t buy it), a YouTube video, and this excellent number theory class lecture.

item.title: Distributional Statistics of Log Values in Linear Domain
item.author: garfieldnate <at> gmail <dot> com (Nathan Glenn)
item.description: Quite a long title, sorry. Basically, I had a bunch of log values (and they needed to stay logarithmic to avoid underflow) and I wanted to compute distributional statistics on them, like mean, variance and kurtosis. I wasn’t sure if it would be valid to compute these kinds of statistics on the numbers as is, so I created a class to do all of the calculations in linear space. I’ll post the result here, though there may be bugs (tell me if you find some!
